session.session_title: PI-gamma build-closure — identity, authentication & RBAC (Production Multi-Tenant API)
session.session_executive_summary: PI-gamma (identity / authentication / RBAC, PRJ-019) built + closed on pi-gamma-rbac, merged FF to main, with the three PI-beta follow-ons. Every request now carries an authenticated principal (human or AI service agent) whose per-engagement roles compose with the row-level engagement scope. New principals/api_tokens/role_assignments tables; a resolve_principal bearer chokepoint + outermost middleware; an RBAC guard + engagement-selection gate; change_log principal attribution; agents as scoped service principals; an owner-gated /admin surface + a crmbuilder-v2-token CLI. Gated by principal_auth_enabled (default OFF -> a synthetic default-owner), so the localhost flow is unchanged. Part A cleared the three follow-ons. Records DEC-379 and resolves PI-127.
session.session_description: PI-gamma build-closure: ingests the eleven commits since the PI-beta closure (the kickoff prompt, the three PI-beta follow-ons A1/A2/A3, the PI-gamma Architecture pass, and the six-slice RBAC build plus a migration-test fixup), records the build as DEC-379, and resolves PI-127. PI-gamma was built directly (no ADO phase decomposition), so there is no workstream-completion step. Merged FF to main at 059d046.

conversation.conversation_purpose: Close out PI-gamma: ingest the build commits, record the build decision, and resolve PI-127.
conversation.conversation_description: Built the PI-beta follow-ons + the six-slice RBAC substrate (principals/tokens/roles, resolve_principal middleware, RBAC enforcement, change_log attribution, agent service principals, admin surface + MCP token) and authored this build-closure.
conversation.conversation_summary: Resolved PI-127; recorded DEC-379; ingested the eleven commits since the PI-beta closure.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: kickoff prompt — PI-beta follow-ons (MCP engagement / drop export_dir column / enumerate_commits) then PI-gamma RBAC
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: kickoff prompt — PI-beta follow-ons (MCP engagement / drop export_dir column / enumerate_commits) then PI-gamma RBAC

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta follow-on A3 — repoint enumerate_commits off deleted db-export snapshot
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta follow-on A3 — repoint enumerate_commits off deleted db-export snapshot

The last-ingested-SHA source defaults to the live REST API
(GET /commits?commit_repository=...&sort=commit_committed_at&order=desc,
X-Engagement header) instead of the deleted db-export/commits.json tree.
--engagement-db-export remains an offline opt-in; an unreachable API warns
and degrades to full history rather than erroring. Adds --api-base /
--engagement flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta follow-on A1 — MCP per-engagement selection
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta follow-on A1 — MCP per-engagement selection

The MCP server names the active engagement on its REST calls via the
X-Engagement header, mirroring the desktop. New CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_ENGAGEMENT
config default is sent on every call when set (empty => unscoped, correct
for the single-engagement dogfood). New select_engagement / get_active_engagement
tools override/read it per session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-beta follow-on A2 — drop vestigial engagement_export_dir column + UI
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-beta follow-on A2 — drop vestigial engagement_export_dir column + UI

PI-beta removed the snapshot/export machinery and the write-time export gate,
leaving engagement_export_dir a dead column nothing reads. This is the
deferred schema-change pass: drop the column, model field, _validate_export_dir,
and all UI for it.

- access: drop the ORM column (models.py), the Engagement dataclass field
  (engagement_models.py), _validate_export_dir + create/update/patch plumbing
  (engagement.py).
- api: drop the field from the three engagement schemas + the create/replace
  router calls.
- migrations: SQLite chain 0040 (batch drop_column) + PG chain
  0002_drop_engagement_export_dir (inspector-guarded, no-op on a fresh baseline).
- ui: drop the export-dir CRUD field + directory-browser/emphasis/confirm
  affordances (engagement_crud, _engagement_schema), the ExportDirWarningBand
  + panel wiring + detail row (panels/engagements), the dead export-dir
  write-gate error path (base/crud_dialog), the connection-info Export
  directory row, and the field from the engagement hydration constructors
  (main_window, new_engagement_dialog).
- tests: drop the export-dir-specific dialog/panel tests and the kwarg from
  Engagement constructors.

Suite green (2183 passed); the only failures are the documented pre-existing
18 (test_0037 catalog-chain-from-scratch + 17 apply_close_out branch-guard,
expected off main).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma Architecture/scoping pass — identity, authentication & RBAC
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma Architecture/scoping pass — identity, authentication & RBAC

PI-gamma's Architecture phase (refines program design D2/D3/D4/D5-final) for
PRJ-019, grounded in a survey of the current auth/middleware/access surface (the
REST API is unauthenticated today; scope_middleware is the chokepoint pattern;
change_log.actor + claimed_by half-implement agent identity; the MCP OAuth AS is
separate).

Decisions: principals + hashed api_tokens (system/shared tables, both migration
chains); a resolve_principal chokepoint + bearer middleware mirroring the
engagement-scope ContextVar, outermost so the engagement selection validates
against the principal's allowed_engagements; roles + per-engagement
role_assignments with a thin require(permission) guard composed with the scope;
ADO agents as scoped service principals (orchestrator mints tokens at spawn,
claimed_by/change_log become real principal references); per-request engagement
validated against the principal. Auth gated behind principal_auth_enabled
(default off -> default owner principal) so the single-operator localhost flow is
unbroken; OIDC/SSO deferred as an additive issuer (the shelved MCP OAuth AS /
Cloudflare setup plugs in later). 6-slice build order. Sequencing: depends on
PI-alpha, composes with PI-beta -> land alpha -> beta -> gamma.

Branch pi-gamma-rbac off main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma slice 1 — principals/api_tokens/role_assignments schema + access layer
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma slice 1 — principals/api_tokens/role_assignments schema + access layer

System/shared identity+RBAC substrate (PI-127, auth off by default):
- models: PrincipalRow / ApiTokenRow / RoleAssignmentRow (plain Base, no
  engagement_id discriminator; role_assignments carries an engagement FK).
- vocab: PRINCIPAL_KINDS/STATUSES, RBAC_ROLES/PERMISSIONS, ROLE_PERMISSIONS map.
- access/principal.py: create_principal, mint/validate/revoke token (SHA-256
  hashed, plaintext shown once), assign_role, _build_principal, get_or_create_owner,
  mint_agent_principal (D-gamma4 orchestrator helper).
- access/principal_scope.py: Principal dataclass + _active_principal ContextVar
  + DEFAULT_OWNER synthetic principal (auth-off path).
- access/rbac.py: principal_permissions / has_permission / check() guard, no-op
  when principal_auth_enabled off.
- config: principal_auth_enabled (default False).
- migrations: SQLite 0041 + PG 0003 (both inspector-guarded for the
  create_all-then-upgrade-head test path).

Tests: access/test_principal.py (13) + migration/test_0041 (2), all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma slice 2 — resolve_principal + bearer middleware (auth off by default)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma slice 2 — resolve_principal + bearer middleware (auth off by default)

- api/principal_middleware.py: pure-ASGI PrincipalMiddleware + resolve_principal
  chokepoint. Reads Authorization: Bearer, validates via the access layer, sets
  the _active_principal ContextVar. Auth off => synthetic DEFAULT_OWNER (zero
  tokens, unchanged behavior). Auth on => 401 envelope on missing/invalid token
  (except the health/docs/root allowlist).
- main.py: add PrincipalMiddleware AFTER EngagementScopeMiddleware so it is
  outermost (principal set before engagement resolution).
- tests: api/test_principal_middleware.py (5). API+MCP suites green (auth-off
  transparent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma slice 3 — RBAC enforcement (engagement-selection gate + require guard)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma slice 3 — RBAC enforcement (engagement-selection gate + require guard)

- scope_middleware: when auth on, validate the selected X-Engagement against
  the active principal's allowed_engagements (403 engagement_forbidden);
  skipped for a None principal (public/allowlisted paths) and unscoped requests.
- api/principal_deps.py: require_permission(perm) FastAPI dependency composing
  rbac.check with the active engagement + principal (no-op when auth off).
- errors.py + main.py: PermissionDenied -> 403 envelope handler.
- test_0038: identify scoped tables by EngagementScopedMixin membership, not by
  the presence of an engagement_id column (role_assignments legitimately carries
  an engagement FK without being a row-scoped tenant table).
- tests: engagement-selection 403 + require_permission, all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma slice 4 — change_log principal attribution + claim-identity guard
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma slice 4 — change_log principal attribution + claim-identity guard

- change_log: principal_id soft-reference column (plain string, not a FK, so the
  append-only audit log outlives a deleted principal); emit() now stamps actor
  (user/service_agent) + principal_id from the active principal. Default-owner
  (auth off) and no-principal callers keep actor=claude_session / principal_id
  NULL, so existing attribution is unchanged.
- vocab: CHANGE_LOG_ACTORS gains service_agent + user.
- work_tasks claim/release: enforce_claim_identity — an agent may only claim as
  itself when auth is on (owner bypass); no-op when auth off.
- migrations: SQLite 0042 + PG 0004 (add column + rebuild actor CHECK, guarded).
- tests: attribution (6) + migration round-trip (2), all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma slices 5-6 — agent service principals + admin surface + MCP token
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma slices 5-6 — agent service principals + admin surface + MCP token

- api/routers/principals.py: owner-gated /admin surface — create principals,
  assign roles, mint/list/revoke tokens (plaintext returned once), disable
  principals, and POST /admin/agents (the ADO orchestrator's D-gamma4 agent-mint:
  engagement-scoped service principal + token in one call). require_permission(
  'admin') gates all routes (no-op auth-off so the localhost flow can provision
  the first owner).
- mcp_server: forward Authorization: Bearer from CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_TOKEN (no-op
  auth-off); config.mcp_token added.
- cli: crmbuilder-v2-token (bootstrap-owner / mint / list / revoke) over the
  access layer directly (works before any token exists); registered in pyproject.
- tests: admin endpoints + owner-gating + MCP forwarding (4), all green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-gamma — pin migration 0041/0042 tests to explicit revisions
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-gamma — pin migration 0041/0042 tests to explicit revisions

upgrade head + downgrade -1 broke once 0042 became head (0041's single-step
downgrade then only undid 0042). Target explicit revisions so the per-migration
round-trip tests stay isolated from later chain growth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: PI-gamma identity/auth/RBAC + PI-beta follow-ons — build decisions and findings
decisions.context: PRJ-019's Production Multi-Tenant API program: PI-alpha (Postgres, Resolved), PI-beta (de-file + kill snapshots, Resolved/PI-126), and PI-gamma (RBAC, PI-127). PI-123 made the data multi-tenant and PI-beta made it a file-free Postgres-capable service; PI-gamma makes it multi-user. This closure also clears the three follow-ons PI-beta intentionally deferred.
decisions.decision: (1) Roles are a fixed vocabulary (RBAC_ROLES + ROLE_PERMISSIONS) rather than a table; role_assignments(principal_id, engagement_id, role) carries the per-engagement grant with a CHECK on role. (2) principals + api_tokens are system/shared tables (no engagement_id discriminator); api_tokens stores only a SHA-256 hash of the high-entropy bearer (plaintext shown once), keeping validation an O(1) indexed lookup. (3) resolve_principal is the single auth chokepoint; PrincipalMiddleware is pure-ASGI and OUTERMOST (added after the engagement middleware) so the principal is set before engagement resolution. (4) RBAC composes in two places: the scope middleware rejects an X-Engagement outside the principal's allowed_engagements (403), and require_permission()/rbac.check() guards operations. (5) change_log gains a principal_id SOFT reference (plain string, not a FK, so the append-only log outlives a deleted principal) + actor kinds user/service_agent; claim/release enforce that an agent claims only as itself. (6) Agents are engagement-scoped service principals: mint_agent_principal + POST /admin/agents mint a principal + token in one call. (7) All gated by principal_auth_enabled (default OFF -> DEFAULT_OWNER). Part A: a CRMBUILDER_V2_MCP_ENGAGEMENT default + select_engagement tool send X-Engagement from MCP; the vestigial engagement_export_dir column + its UI were dropped (SQLite 0040 + PG 0002, inspector-guarded); enumerate_commits reads the last-ingested SHA from the live API by default.
decisions.rationale: PI-123 made the data multi-tenant and PI-alpha/PI-beta made it a Postgres service with no file apparatus; PI-gamma makes it multi-USER along the same query path. Mirroring the engagement-scope ContextVar/middleware pattern keeps the principal layer a thin, familiar composition. Default-OFF auth preserves the single-operator localhost flow and keeps the whole suite green, while the substrate is ready to turn on in the deployed managed-PG service. A future OIDC/SSO issuer is a second implementation feeding the same Principal — the abstraction is the point. PI-122 (Agent Profile Registry) consumes this principal model + the system|engagement scope.
decisions.alternatives_considered: A roles table (rejected for now: the role set is small and fixed; a vocabulary + CHECK is simpler and the registry/UI can drive from RBAC_ROLES). A hard FK from change_log to principals (rejected: an append-only audit log must outlive a deleted principal; SET NULL erases attribution and CASCADE erases history, so a soft string reference is correct). A stretched KDF for tokens (rejected: tokens are 256-bit random secrets, so SHA-256 is the standard hash and preserves O(1) lookup). Rejecting unauthenticated requests inside every router (rejected: the middleware 401 with a public allowlist is the single honest gate; fine-grained per-entity permissions are deferred per 'start coarse').
decisions.consequences: Multi-user auth is in place but inert until principal_auth_enabled is turned on (deployed managed-PG). Build finding load-bearing for the closure: the live unified DB is create_all-managed (no alembic_version row), so the PI-gamma schema delta was applied to it directly via create_all (the three RBAC tables) + ALTER change_log ADD COLUMN principal_id, rather than alembic upgrade; the actor-CHECK widening and the engagement_export_dir drop are deferred on the live DB (harmless with auth off and the access layer no longer referencing the column). Both Alembic chains (SQLite 0040-0042, PG 0002-0004) carry the full schema for fresh builds. Deferred: the ADO orchestrator runtime that mints/spawns agents (PI-122), OIDC/SSO, and finer-grained permissions.
decisions.executive_summary: Records PI-gamma's build: principals + hashed api_tokens + per-engagement role_assignments (roles as a fixed vocabulary, not a table); a resolve_principal bearer chokepoint with an outermost pure-ASGI middleware mirroring the engagement scope; RBAC enforced as an engagement-selection gate plus a require_permission guard; change_log principal attribution via a soft principal_id reference; agents as engagement-scoped service principals (mint_agent_principal + /admin/agents); an owner-gated /admin surface and a crmbuilder-v2-token CLI. Gated by principal_auth_enabled (default OFF -> a synthetic default-owner). Also clears the three PI-beta follow-ons. Build finding: the live DB is create_all-managed, so its schema delta was applied directly.
